title: rhinanthus glacialis subsp. subalpinus
intro: Rhinanthus glacialis subsp. subalpinus, also known as the alpine witch's thimble or snow primrose, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Rhinanthus, belonging to the family Orobanchaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Rhinanthus glacialis subsp. subalpinus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
